description_long: This object is a small, ancient-looking blade, likely an arrowhead or a dagger blade, with a rough, weathered surface and a dark, mottled coloration. The blade is double-edged and tapers to a point at both ends, with a slightly thicker midsection and a flattened profile. Its surface appears pitted and irregular, suggesting it is made from forged or cast metal, possibly iron or bronze, and has experienced significant corrosion or aging. One side of the blade features a visible marking or inscription in white, possibly an inventory or catalog number. There is no visible handle, guard, or decorative elements, and the object appears to be a utilitarian tool or weapon fragment. The overall form is compact and robust, suitable for piercing or cutting tasks.
description: A small, double-edged, weathered metal blade with a rough, pitted surface and dark coloration, likely an ancient tool or weapon fragment.
description_view_0: A narrow edge-on view showing the thin profile and pointed ends of the blade.
description_view_1: A side view revealing the full length, double-edged shape, and rough, mottled surface texture.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view, similar to view 0, emphasizing the blade's thinness and symmetry.
description_view_3: A side view with visible white markings or inscriptions, highlighting the blade's midsection and surface irregularities.
attribute_handle_design: no visible handle, blade extends throughout
